properties.remark: FLOODING AND MUDSLIDES WERE REPORTED NEAR RINCON RANCH ROAD. VEHCILE STUCK IN MUD.
DE LUZ ROAD IMPASSABLE DUE TO HEAVY RAIN AND FLASH FLOODING. DE LUZ ROAD COMPLETELY COVERED WITH DEBRIS.
93 MPH GUST 1230 PM MEASURED BY LIFEGUARDS AT NEWPORT BEACH PIER. ALSO MEASURED GUSTS TO 72 AND 73 MPH.
A WATERSPOUT WAS SIGHTED OFF THE COAST BY THE WEATHER OBSERVER AT JOHN WAYNE AIRPORT. TIME AND LOCATION IS ESTIMATED.
A 92 MPH WIND GUST WAS MEASURED BY LIFEGUARDS AT THE HUNTINGTON BEACH PIER. TIME OF WIND GUST ESTIMATED.

TORNADO SPOTTED ON PACIFIC COAST HIGHWAY JUST SOUTH OF ANDERSON STREET. THE FUNNEL CROSSED THE HIGHWAY AND HEADED NE. A BLACK LIMO WAS LIFTED UP AND AN SUV WAS FLIPPED AND THROWN INTO A TREE. DOWNED TREES AND ROOF DAMAGE WERE ALSO SEEN IN THE AREA.
STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS...POSSIBLY A TORNADO...TORE OFF PART OF THE ROOF A BUILDING IN AN INDUSTRIAL PARK AND DAMAGED NEARBY MOBILE HOMES.
A PEAK WIND GUST OF 60 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE FREMONT CANYON RAWS.
DAMAGE TO MOBILE HOMES REPORTED BY ORANGE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT
WIND DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS REPORTED IN SEAL BEACH BY ORANGE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.
PROBABLE TORNADO IN HUNTINGTON BEACH. BOATS UPLIFTED AND DAMAGED IN THE HB HARBOR. DAMAGE TO BUILDING IN AREAS. CAR FLIPPED 16400 PCH.
A 60 MPH WIND GUST WAS MEASURED BY LIFEGUARDS AT SAN CLEMENTE PIER.
A PEAK WIND GUST OF 59 MPH WAS MEASURED BY THE SAN MIGUEL RAWS.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER ESTIMATED WINDS IN EXCESS OF 60 MPH AT SAN DIEGO COUNTRY ESTATES.
A PEAK THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 62 MPH WAS MEASURED ON MOUNT WOODSON.
TWO LARGE TREES DOWN BLOCKING ROADWAY NEAR SAN DIEGO COUNTRY ESTATES.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 44 MPH RECORDED AT THE BORREGO SPRINGS MESONET OB AT 2245Z. 5 TELEPHONE POLES KNOCKED DOWN ON BORREGO SPRINGS ROAD. ROOF OF UNKNOWN STRUCTURE TORN OFF.
SEVERAL ROADS UNDER 1-2 FEET OF WATER IN EL MIRAGE. LOW SECTIONS OF HIGHWAY 395 ALSO FLOODED.
OVERTURNED BIG RIG ON I-15 NEAR THE LENWOOD EXIT. ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS 55-60 MPH AND QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
TREES, BOULDERS, MUD FLOATING DOWN VALLEY OF THE FALLS ROAD.
TEN POWER POLES KNOCKED DOWN, LIVE POWER LINES FELL ONTO TO RTA BUS TRAPPING DRIVER AND 11 PASSENGERS FOR TWO HOURS. STREET FLOODING IN LAKE ELSINORE.
HIGHWAY 74 CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING UNTIL 1600 PDT
SPOTTER SDG173 ESTIMATES WIND GUSTS 50-60 MPH
SPOTTER SDG173 OBSERVED QUARTER INCH HAIL
SENECA ROAD FROM AMARGOSA ROAD TO EL EVADO ROAD COMPLETELY WASHED AWAY.
1.8 INCHES OF RAIN MEASURED IN LESS THAN ONE HOUR FROM 1300-1356 PDT. MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS SIDE ROADS AND HIGHWAY 79. REPORTED BY SPOTTER SDG173.
MUD, DEBRIS, AND ROCKS BLOCKING HIGHWAY 79. SPOTTER REPORTED THAT LARGE HAIL REPORTED EARLIER, FROM 1254-1259 PDT.
MATAGUATY SDGE ANEMOMETER LOCATED AT 6 METER AGL MEASURED PEAK WINDS AT 1330 PDT NNW 30 MPH GUSTING 60 MPH. DOWNDRAFTS IN EXCESS OF 40 MPH STARTED SHORTLY AFTER 1320 AND CONTINUED UNTIL 1350 PDT
DOWNED LARGE TREES 1 TO 2 FEET IN DIAMETER. ALSO DOWNED POWER LINES. WINDS ESTIMATED FROM 60-70 MPH.
TREES BLOWN DOWN, MARBLE SIZE HAIL, REPORTED BY SPOTTER RIV197

HEAVY RAIN FLOODED HIGHWAY 18 AND 247. 2 TO 3 FEET OF WATER OVER ROADS. TWO SWIFT WATER RESCUES IN THE VICINITY. THREE PEOPLE IN A CAR WERE WASHED AWAY BUT WERE SAVED. 1.73 INCHES OF RAIN FELL BETWEEN 3PM AND 430 PM.
APPROXIMATELY 6 INCHES OF WATER WITH DEBRIS ON ROADWAY AT THE INTERSECTION OF I-15 AND HIGHWAY 18.
BLOWING SAND REDUCING VISIBILITY ACROSS HIGHWAY 55 NEAR DYER ROAD
WIND BLOWING DUST ACROSSS INTERSTATE 10 NEAR ETIWANDA AVENUE, VISIBILITY NEAR ZERO
VISIBILITY 0.75 MILE IN BLOWING DUST, WIND SUSTAINED 20 MPH WITH GUSTS 38 MPH
FLASH FLOODING ALONG LYTLE CREEK RUNNING OVER GLEN HELEN PARKWAY, NEAR INTERSTATE 15 NORTHEAST OF FONTANA. WATER WAS WITHIN ONE FOOT OF A LEVEE.
FLASH FLOOD OCCURRED ALONG LYTLE CREEK AROUND 600 PM. ONE MOTORIST HAD TO BE RESCUED WHEN TRAPPED IN HIS CAR IN HIGH WATER.
FLASH FLOOD OCCURRED IN MIRA LOMA AROUND 6 PM REQUIRING 2 DIFFERENT SWIFT-WATER RESCUES OF 4 PEOPLE TOTAL.
FLASH FLOODING AT THE 60 FREEWAY AND RUBIDOUX--5 PEOPLE HAD TO BE RESCUED.
WIDESPREAD URBAN FLOODING/PONDING. MISC STALLED VEHICALES. NAVAJO ROAD CLOSED AT OTTAWA DUE TO FLOODING.
STREET FLOODING IN CORONADO. BOTH NORTHBOUND LANES ON ORANGE AVE BLVD.WITH 3 TO 4 INCHES OF WATER. 6 TO 7 INCHES OF WATER ON 1ST STREET NEAR FERRY LANDING
FLOODING IN TRABUCO CREEK WITH 5 RESCUES REPORTED IN TRABUCO CANYON. NO INJURIES REPORTED AT THIS TIME.
ONE SWIFTWATER RESCUE THIS MORNING IN WRIGHTWOOD.
FLOOD CHANNEL OVERFLOWED RESULTING IN EVACUATIONS OF 12 HOUSES IN BARRIO LOGAN NEIGHBORHOOD. 4 TO 5 PEOPLE WERE EVACUATED FROM HOMES IN ENCANTO DUE TO STREAM FLOODING.
EXTENSIVE FLOODING IN DOWNTOWN LAGUNA BEACH WITH 4 FEET OF WATER ON BROADWAY STREET. OFFICIALS RESCUED STRANDED MOTORISTS.
LOMA LINDA FD/EM REPORTS FLASH FLOOD/DEBRIS FLOW/LANDSLIDE. SEVERAL VEHICLESTRAPPED. RESCUES IN PROGRESS.
MANY STREETS FLOODED THROUGHOUT APPLE VALLEY. HOMES AND BUSINESSES FLOODED. MANY VEHICLES DISABLED.
STREAMS HAVE FLOODED ROADS INTO AND OUT OF PIONEERTOWN. STORM TOTAL RAINFALL IS 5.00 INCHES.
FLASH FLOOD ALONG LYTLE CREEK ROAD. SWIFT WATER RESCUE OF MOTORIST PERFORMED BY FIRE DEPT.
